texto: My dad is Scottish, and he read in the newspaper about the plight of the Scottish Freshwater Mussel, which is a real thing - like, a very real, serious conservation issue. And he's a writer, and he was going to do a film about a Glaswegian gangster, and then I stole the idea and turned it into a romantic comedy.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Talulah Riley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “My dad is Scottish, and he read in the newspaper about the plight of the Scottish Freshwater Muss…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, gangster, newspaper pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de newspaper e comedy.

texto: I have a checklist of things I'd like to do in movies. One of them is get possessed. Die on a deathbed with a ghostly pallor - that's on my list.
